texto: I think I was always interested in the larger world, even as a kid, and my experiences as a journalist only heightened that interest. Covering conflict, I learned that though leaders often try to create a sense of "us" and "them," the differences are not that delineated. I often felt like it was a whole bunch of "us," with some of "them" scattered around. That made me feel that the borders we draw around ourselves are often artificial.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Masha Hamilton: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de temas ligados a lideranca. O trecho “I think I was always interested in the larger world, even as a kid, and my experiences as a journ…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que lideranca, journalism, multiculturalism pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de multiculturalism.

I often felt like it was a whole bunch of "us," with some of "them" scattered around. That made me feel that the borders we draw around ourselves are often artificial.

texto: The stories she'd read of others' lives over these last few months had left her with a greater appreciation for the thread of her own life.
explicacao: Citação de Masha Hamilton sobre sentido da existência e experiência vivida. “The stories she'd read of others' lives over these last few months had left her with a greater ap…” articula reflexao, reading-books, stories em registro de reflexao, com relevância para contextos como reflexao.

texto: Have you heard," he said "that many of our people believe if you know five colloquial expressions in their tribal language, they must always provide you with nourishment and shelter? But-" He paused as though to make sure she was paying attention. "But if you know fewer than five, they owe you not even a sip of water."She nodded, understanding his point, but he pressed it."Learn those five phrases, Miss Sweeney," he said.
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de Masha Hamilton que provoca temas ligados a sabedoria. Ao questionar “Have you heard," he said "that many of our people believe if you know five colloquial expressions…”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre sabedoria, culture, hospitality, com foco em hospitality e language.

But-" He paused as though to make sure she was paying attention. "But if you know fewer than five, they owe you not even a sip of water."She nodded, understanding his point, but he pressed it."Learn those five phrases, Miss Sweeney," he said.

texto: Besides, Fi was convinced that instinct could determine a body's literary needs, just as physical cravings pointed to dietary shortfalls. She'd experienced it herself more than once among the library's dense shelves; not knowing what she should read next, she'd wandered, sniffing slightly, palms open. When intuition hit, she felt a sensation she couldn't describe exactly: her hands seemed to know where to go. And when she reached, invariably she found exactly the book she needed at that moment - sometimes fiction, sometimes biography, sometimes a slim volume of obscure poetry
explicacao: Orientação direta de Masha Hamilton sobre passagem do tempo e prioridades. A afirmação “Besides, Fi was convinced that instinct could determine a body's literary needs, just as physical…” funciona como lembrete prático ligado a tempo, books, library, útil em momentos de library.
